title: lonchocarpus schubertiae
intro: Lonchocarpus schubertiae, also known as the "Pau Ferrao" or "Palo de Hierba", is a medicinal plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Lonchocarpus schubertiae have been reported to provide health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as the reduction of inflammation and pain. Herbal preparations of Lonchocarpus schubertiae can be made by infusing the leaves and bark in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and grinding the plant material into a powder for use in capsules or tinctures.
